behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index
behaviors.justification: A mock embedding model could always return vectors of the 'correct' dimensionality, completely hiding dimension mismatches between code and the real embedding API. The real failure occurs when the embedding model's actual output dimension doesn't match what the code/config expects (e.g., embedding model was upgraded from 384 to 768 dims, or API client is misconfigured). Testing requires calling the real embedding model to catch model-version incompatibilities and config drift that infrastructure mocks would hide.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model (e.g., OpenAI, Sentence Transformers) with a test query. Verify the returned vector has exactly N dimensions matching the vector database schema. Run with multiple query types and verify consistency. Verify dimension matches config at startup.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score
behaviors.justification: A mock vector database could always return results in the 'correct' descending order, hiding bugs in query construction, vector formatting, or vector database configuration. The real failure occurs when actual vector DB behavior (index corruption, API returning unexpected sort order, similarity calculation bugs in the DB, wrong metric selected) produces incorrect ranking. Testing requires a real vector database instance to catch ranking inversions, configuration mismatches, and metric-selection errors that mocks would hide.
behaviors.test_description: Insert vectors of known relative distances into a real vector database instance. Query with a test vector and retrieve top-10 results. Verify results are strictly ordered by descending similarity score. Use pre-calculated or manually-verifiable similarity pairs (e.g., perpendicular vectors score lowest, parallel vectors score highest) to make ordering testable.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent
behaviors.justification: A mock LLM could always return any ordering claimed to be 'correct' without demonstrating that the model actually understood the query's nuance. The real failure occurs when the re-ranking prompt is poorly written (query intent ignored), the LLM simply echoes raw similarity order without reordering, or the system generates nonsensical rankings. Testing requires a real LLM call to verify the prompt actually triggers meaningful reordering in response to nuanced requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Send a multi-part, nuanced query (e.g., 'affordable, lightweight, durable laptop under $1000') through the full ranking pipeline. Call the real LLM re-ranking step on a curated set of search results. Verify that at least some results are reordered compared to raw similarity ranking. Run multiple times (3-5) to account for LLM non-determinism and verify re-ranking is reproducibly different, not random.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise
behaviors.justification: A mock vector database could apply any threshold 'correctly', hiding the real issue: that threshold values are misaligned with actual similarity scores returned by the real database. The real failure occurs when the similarity threshold is set wrong (e.g., hardcoded 0.9 threshold but realistic matches score 0.3–0.5 in production), the similarity metric is different than assumed, or threshold logic is missing. Testing requires a real vector database to understand actual similarity value ranges and confirm threshold logic filters correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Insert vectors into a real vector database with known similar and dissimilar pairs. Query with a low similarity threshold such that some results fall below it. Verify: (1) results below threshold are excluded, (2) if all results fall below threshold, an empty set is returned (not the lowest-scoring result). Repeat with different thresholds to verify boundary conditions.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case
behaviors.justification: A unit test with mocked embedding models, vector databases, and LLMs would always pass instantly, completely missing real latency bottlenecks. The real failure occurs when actual service latencies (embedding API response time: 100–500ms, vector DB query time: 50–200ms, LLM inference time: 500–2000ms) or network overhead cause the pipeline to exceed the 2-second budget. Testing requires calling all real services in sequence and measuring actual wall-clock latency. Although the LLM introduces non-determinism in response time, the core test is system-level latency orchestration, not LLM behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send 20+ diverse real queries through the full pipeline (embedding → similarity search → LLM re-ranking) using all real services. Measure end-to-end latency for each query (from request received to results returned). Calculate median latency and verify it is below 2 seconds. Log component latencies separately (embedding, vector DB query, re-ranking) to identify bottlenecks and catch regressions in any stage.
